2606.08247.title: AeroSpectra Sentinel: An Auditable LLM Prompt-Chaining Decision-Support Workflow for Acute Asthma Risk Assessment from Respiratory Sounds and Clinical Signals
2606.08247.paper_summary: Acute asthma risk assessment requires rapid interpretation of respiratory sounds, oxygenation, airflow limitation, speech ability, work of breathing, mental status, and response to reliever therapy. Conventional audio-only classifiers can detect wheeze-like patterns but often lack transparent clinical reasoning and safe escalation logic. This paper presents AeroSpectra Sentinel, a client-side research prototype and decision-support workflow that combines short-time Fourier transform (STFT) respiratory sound analysis, lightweight machine-learning screening, clinical feature fusion, and a five-stage large language model (LLM) prompt-chaining process. The workflow separates signal acquisition, preprocessing, acoustic feature extraction, ML screening, clinical guardrails, and FHIR-ready reporting. We evaluated the audio screening component on a public respiratory sound dataset containing 1,211 WAV recordings from five labels. Using a stratified subset of 584 recordings, a random forest achieved 91.10% binary accuracy and 78.69% F1-score for asthma-vs-non-asthma screening, while a feature-based multilayer perceptron achieved 89.73% accuracy and 78.26% F1-score. A compact log-spectrogram CNN achieved 73.29% accuracy and 55.17% F1-score. Multiclass classification achieved 77.40% accuracy and 77.23% macro-F1. To evaluate the LLM workflow, we conducted a scenario-based audit on 40 simulated clinical vignettes comparing one-shot prompting, prompt chaining, prompt chaining with guardrails, and prompt chaining with guardrails plus FHIR schema validation. The guardrail-plus-schema variant achieved the strongest simulated safety and documentation consistency. AeroSpectra Sentinel is intended as a research prototype, not as a diagnostic medical device or clinically validated risk-assessment product.
2606.08247.paper_summary_zh: 急性哮喘風險評估需要快速解讀呼吸聲音、氧合狀態、氣流限制、語言能力、呼吸工作、心理狀態及對緩解療法的反應。傳統的僅音頻分類器可以檢測類似喘息的模式，但通常缺乏透明的臨床推理和安全升級邏輯。本文介紹了AeroSpectra Sentinel，一個客戶端研究原型和決策支持工作流程，結合了短時傅里葉變換（STFT）呼吸聲音分析、輕量級機器學習篩選、臨床特徵融合以及五階段大型語言模型（LLM）提示鏈接過程。該工作流程將信號獲取、預處理、聲學特徵提取、機器學習篩選、臨床保障和FHIR準備報告分開。 我們在一個包含1,211個來自五個標籤的WAV錄音的公共呼吸聲音數據集上評估了音頻篩選組件。使用584個錄音的分層子集，隨機森林在哮喘與非哮喘篩選中達到了91.10%的二元準確率和78.69%的F1分數，而基於特徵的多層感知器達到了89.73%的準確率和78.26%的F1分數。一個緊湊的對數頻譜CNN達到了73.29%的準確率和55.17%的F1分數。多類別分類達到了77.40%的準確率和77.23%的宏觀F1分數。為了評估LLM工作流程，我們對40個模擬臨床小插曲進行了基於情境的審計，比較了一次性提示、提示鏈接、帶保障的提示鏈接，以及帶保障加FHIR架構驗證的提示鏈接。帶保障加架構變體達到了最強的模擬安全性和文檔一致性。AeroSpectra Sentinel旨在作為研究原型，而非診斷醫療設備或臨床驗證的風險評估產品。
2606.08247.author: Aueaphum Aueawatthanaphisut et.al.
